body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest
July 3, 2026
Circle shares dropped 17.55% after 140+ firms (Visa, Stripe, BlackRock, Coinbase) launched the rival Open USD stablecoin, as GENIUS Act final rules loom on July 18.
Stablecoin News
Visa, Stripe, BlackRock, Coinbase launch Open USD (OUSD) stablecoin

140+ companies including Mastercard, BNY and Standard Chartered launched OUSD on Solana with free issuance/redemption and reserve revenue shared back to distributors, undercutting Circle and Tether's retained-yield model.
GENIUS Act final stablecoin rules due July 18

Six federal agencies are finalizing GENIUS Act rules after comment periods closed June 9; non-bank fintechs like Stripe and Block must raise $5M in dedicated capital and charter a stablecoin bank or exit the market.
USDT market cap hits all-time high near $188B, widening lead over USDC
Tether's dominance has grown since Circle was criticized for slow freezing of USDC linked to the Drift Protocol exploit; USDC sits at roughly $78.25B.
Regulators propose bank-grade KYC rules for stablecoin issuers
Five US regulators jointly proposed stricter KYC requirements under the GENIUS Act, putting both Circle's USDC and Tether's USDT under closer scrutiny.
Hacking & Security Incidents
Syscoin Bridge exploited for ~$10M
An attacker exploited a proof-validation flaw to mint roughly 5 billion unauthorized SYS tokens, one of the larger DeFi bridge incidents of June 2026.
Humanity Protocol drained of $31-36M via compromised founder key
Attackers gained control after a private key linked to founder Terence Kwok was compromised on a malware-infected machine, then minted and drained the project's H token across two networks.
H1 2026 sets record for hack count, North Korea tied to 66% of stolen funds
TRM Labs counted 207 crypto hacking incidents in H1 2026 with losses near $972M-$1B; roughly two-thirds of stolen funds are attributed to North Korea-linked actors.
Wasabi Protocol drained of $4.5M in admin key compromise
An apparent admin key compromise let attackers siphon $4.5M from the protocol, part of a wider pattern of private-key and key-management failures driving 2026 losses.
